name: Ruta del Visón Trail
name_original: Peralta a Falces – circular por el río Arga
description: Hike the 8.7-mile Ruta del Visón Trail, a moderate, family-friendly route along the Arga and Aragón rivers, known for wildlife.
region_intro: Hiking around Peralta offers diverse trails through a landscape shaped by rivers and ravines. The region features paths along the Rio Arga and Rio Aragón, providing scenic waterside routes. Hikers can explore areas with gentle elevations, including the Atalaya de Peralta, which offers views from a historic watchtower. The terrain primarily consists of riverine environments and open fields, with some moderate ascents. Best hiking trails around Peralta The most popular hiking route is Barranco del Rey – circular desde Funes, a 7.4 miles (11.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 18 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Barranco del Rey ravine. Another top favourite among local hikers is Barranco del Rey from Funes via the Rio Arga, an easy 4.0 miles (6.4 km) path. This trail follows the Rio Arga, offering riverside scenery. Local hikers also love the Ruta del Visón Trail, a 8.7 miles (14.0 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Hiking around Peralta is defined by river paths, open fields, and gentle hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside strolls to moderate loops. The routes in Peralta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 25 reviews. More than 180 hikers have used komoot to explore Peralta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Peralta?
A: Peralta offers a variety of trails, primarily featuring riverine environments along the Rio Arga and Rio Aragón, as well as open fields. You'll find routes with gentle elevations, including paths leading to the Atalaya de Peralta, and options ranging from easy riverside strolls to moderate loops through ravines.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for families in Peralta?
A: Yes, Peralta has many easy trails perfect for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970779767/barranco-del-rey-from-funes-via-the-rio-arga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Rey from Funes via the Rio Arga&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.4 km path that follows the river, offering pleasant scenery. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1106404277/atalaya-de-peralta-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalaya de Peralta Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a shorter 3.2 km route with gentle ascents.

Q: What are some circular hiking routes available in Peralta?
A: Peralta features several excellent circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103051433/barranco-del-rey-circular-desde-funes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco del Rey – circular desde Funes&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 11.8 km trail exploring the ravine. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1112818825/las-boyas-del-campo-a-rio-aragon-circular-desde-marcilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Boyas del Campo a río Aragón – circular desde Marcilla&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 14.8 km route.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Peralta?
A: The trails in Peralta are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 25 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse river paths, open fields, and the variety of options for different ability levels, from relaxing riverside walks to more engaging loops.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes in the Peralta area?
A: While Peralta is known for its easy to moderate trails, there is one difficult route among the 45 available. Most trails feature gentle elevations, but some moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1106426361/ruta-del-vison-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Visón Trail&lt;/a&gt; (14.0 km) offer a good workout through riverine landscapes.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Peralta?
A: Given the region's riverine environments along the Rio Arga and Rio Aragón, hikers might spot various bird species, aquatic wildlife, and small mammals typical of riverside habitats and open fields. Keep an eye out, especially during quieter times of day.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions near the hiking trails?
A: Around Peralta, you can explore several notable attractions. Consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2210948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marcilla Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a historic fortress, or the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5259467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calahorra Old Town&lt;/a&gt;. Nature lovers might appreciate the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2791578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalén Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. The settlements of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5692290" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Adrián&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5304112" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Funes&lt;/a&gt; also offer local charm.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Peralta?
A: The best time to hike in Peralta generally depends on personal preference, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring the river paths and open fields. Summers can be warm, while winters might bring cooler, potentially wetter conditions, though many trails remain accessible.

Q: Are there any specific trails that offer good views?
A: Yes, trails that ascend towards the Atalaya de Peralta, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1106404277/atalaya-de-peralta-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atalaya de Peralta Loop&lt;/a&gt;, provide elevated perspectives over the surrounding landscape, including views of the historic watchtower and the river valleys.

Q: How many hiking trails are there in the Peralta region?
A: There are over 45 hiking trails available for exploration in the Peralta region, catering to various skill levels. This includes 25 easy routes, 19 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the hiking areas in Peralta?
A: While specific cafes directly on the trails are uncommon, the nearby towns and settlements like Funes and Marcilla, which serve as starting points for many routes, typically offer local establishments where you can find refreshments and meals after your hike.
tips: Peralta is a charming little town of around 6,000 inhabitants. The city has a long and interesting history and there are several historical sites in and around it.
Little is known about this picturesque bridge over the Arga River, but it is assumed that it dates from Roman times and was the gateway to the ancient settlement that is today the town of Peralta.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ruta del Visón Trail?
faqs.answer: The Ruta del Visón Trail is accessible from multiple towns, including Peralta, Funes, Falces, and Marcilla. You can find parking in the town centers of any of these municipalities, making it convenient to start your hike from various points along the route.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta del Visón Trail suitable for families and beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is very family-friendly and suitable for beginners. It features a flat terrain with no significant elevation gain, making it an easy and accessible option for a relaxed outdoor experience for all ages.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is generally during spring or autumn when the weather is mild and pleasant. These seasons offer comfortable temperatures for hiking and optimal conditions for observing wildlife along the riverbanks.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Ruta del Visón Trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally dog-friendly, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the presence of endangered wildlife like the European mink. This helps protect both your pet and the local ecosystem.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to hike here?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any specific permits or have to pay an entrance fee to hike the Ruta del Visón Trail. However, as the trail is located within the LIC Tramos Bajos del Aragón y del Arga, please respect all local regulations regarding wildlife and natural conservation.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features flat terrain, making for an easy walk. You'll traverse diverse landscapes including Mediterranean scrub forests, rocky outcrops, and riverine woodlands along the Arga and Aragón rivers. The section between Peralta and Falces is particularly scenic with dense vegetation and sculpted rock formations.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I see along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is renowned for being part of "Territorio Visón," a habitat for the endangered European mink. Besides mink, you might spot otters and various bird species, including black-crowned night herons, white storks, little grebes, and even birds of prey like the Egyptian vulture and Eurasian eagle-owl.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as you hike, you'll enjoy picturesque views, especially of the bridge over the Arga River near Peralta. The riverine landscape itself, with its dense vegetation and sculpted rock formations, offers many natural viewpoints.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking or cycling routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Ruta del Visón Trail largely follows the official 'Ruta ciclable del Visón' for about 96% of its length. It also briefly intersects with sections of 'Paso Río Arga'.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the hike?
faqs.answer: The full circular route, which is approximately 14 kilometers, typically takes around 3 hours and 30 minutes to complete at a moderate hiking pace. This duration can vary depending on your speed and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or observe wildlife.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail connects several towns, including Peralta, Funes, Falces, and Marcilla. Each of these municipalities offers amenities such as cafes, pubs, and accommodation options, so you can easily find refreshments or a place to stay before or after your hike.

waypoints.name: Views of the bridge over the Arga River – Peralta
